Football.title: Major change to football calendar with ‘Easter fixtures AXED for Premier League and Championship next season’
Football.content:
FIFA are set to make major changes to the football calendar that will mean Premier League and Championship games are axed on Easter weekend next season.

Officials are set to shift next year’s international break to cover Good Friday and Easter Monday, according to the Daily Mail.





The two week international break in 2027 will start after domestic fixtures have been completed on March 20, the report claims.

International fixtures will then be played across Good Friday on March 26 and Easter Monday on March 29.

However, most national teams will play on the Saturday and Tuesday.

The report adds that domestic action will resume after the break with the FA Cup quarter-finals on April 3.



Officials in the major leagues were not consulted by Fifa over the new scheduling, it says.

English football matches have been a staple of Easter weekend since 1889.

For over a decade, Premier League clubs have played one round of fixtures over the course of the weekend, with EFL clubs playing twice.

This season, the spring international break takes place after the last round of fixtures on March 22.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

A handful of teams like Wales, Northern Ireland and Italy are in World Cup play-off action.

England, meanwhile, face friendlies against Uruguay on March 27 and Japan on March 31.

Football’s governing body, who are preparing for the biggest ever World Cup in the United States, Canada and Mexico this summer, have already made changes to the football calendar.

There will be an extended international break which will see top flight games pause in September 19 and only restart on October 10.



That means international games will be played all in one go this autumn, rather than having two separate breaks in September and October.

Countries won’t play any additional games, but they’ll play four times over that extended break rather than two in September and two in October as usual.

It comes after only one match – Manchester United vs Newcastle – was played on Boxing Day this season.

That was because this season’s Boxing Day fell on a Friday and broadcasting rules require Premier League games to be played on 33 weekends every season.

Football.title: Arsenal star Riccardo Calafiori issues transfer plea after losing place and has already ‘pictured it’
Football.content:
RICCARDO CALAFIORI has claimed he would love to return to former club Roma in future.

Calafiori, 23, is already thinking about his future just two years after joining the Gunners from Bologna in a £42 million transfer.



The versatile defender has been impressive for Arsenal since his 2024 arrival, scoring four goals and registering five assists in 54 appearances for the club.



However, he has recently slipped down the pecking order for Mikel Arteta’s side after some eye-catching performances by Piero Hincapie.

And it appears the Italian’s heart is still set on a romantic return to boyhood club Roma, whom he left in 2022.

Speaking on the Supernova Podcast, Calafiori said: “I would like to return to Roma sooner or later.

“Obviously, we can’t plan our career out now, but yes [I picture it].



“I left Roma with unfinished business. It all started really well and I even had the chance to play a bit more.

“And then, for various reasons, first I went out on loan and then they sold me. I definitely didn’t do what I had hoped.

“So I left something there that I would like to [return to].”



CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

Calafiori came through the academy system at Roma, joining the club as an eight-year-old.

But he managed just 18 first-team appearances for the Giallorossi before being loaned out to Genoa and ultimately sold to FC Basel.

His progression at Roma was stunted by a horror injury when he was just 16.

The full-back ruptured all the ligaments in one of his knees while playing in a Uefa Youth League match, leaving him fearing his career could be over.

Now he is thriving in the Premier League, and although he has his sights set on a return to Italy one day, Calafiori is loving life in the capital.



He lives near central London, far away from the family homes his teammates choose closer to Arsenal’s St Albans training ground.

The Full Arsenal on YouTube SunSport have launched a dedicated Arsenal channel on YouTube! The Full Arsenal Podcast is your home for everything about the Gunners. From match reactions and tactical breakdowns to transfer talk, match previews, tactical analysis and honest fan debate… The Full Arsenal covers it all. Subscribe to The Full Arsenal here

He said: “I live quite far away from my teammates; nobody lives where I live. It’s a London thing.

“Most of them have families and kids and live in houses near the training ground. I’m younger, and on my own, so I prefer living in the city.

“Normally, we check in by 9.15am. It takes about 50 minutes to get there.”

But Calafiori is still getting used to life in the UK and already has issues with the way people drive over here, so much so that he has chosen to go without a car.



He added: “I don’t drive, but I get around with a driver. I prefer not to drive here. I don’t have a car, but I can drive.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“I don’t like how they drive here. They follow the rules too much and are a bit slow.

“Sometimes you see these big traffic jams for no reason. Unfortunately, that’s just how it is here. Everyone has their ways.”

Football.title: Man Utd ‘want Newcastle star Bruno Guimaraes to replace Casemiro’ with Brazilian, 34, keen on joining European giant
Football.content:
MANCHESTER UNITED are eyeing up a blockbuster move for Newcastle ace Bruno Guimaraes as they prepare for life after Casemiro.

The Red Devils are plotting a huge summer rebuild, with Casemiro, 34, moving on after signalling he will not renew his deal following the World Cup.





That leaves a gaping hole in midfield and United are ready to splash the cash to fill it.

According to reports in Spain, the club’s chiefs are understood to be prepared to invest more than £180million in a major overhaul, with the Brazilian’s successor top of the agenda.

Leading the wishlist is Newcastle talisman Bruno Guimaraes, who has impressed in the Premier League with his energy, leadership and big-game performances.



The 28-year-old is viewed at Old Trafford as the ideal long-term replacement for compatriot Casemiro.

However, prising him away from Newcastle United will be no easy task.

The Magpies are in talks over a new contract for Guimaraes and are known as tough negotiators after banking a club-record fee for Alexander Isak last year.

United have made informal contact with representatives but no club-to-club agreement is in place.



Also on the radar is Newcastle midfielder Sandro Tonali, though the Toon would likely only sanction one major departure.

BEST FREE BETS AND BETTING SIGN UP OFFERS

Further options include Elliot Anderson, currently at Nottingham Forest, as well as Adam Wharton of Crystal Palace.

Brazil international Joao Gomes at Wolverhampton Wanderers is another name in the frame if Wolves’ struggles continue.

But it is Guimaraes who tops the list, with United determined to land a proven Premier League star as they look to rebuild their midfield and launch a fresh title charge.



SunSport sources close to Casemiro have flagged Italy as a possible destination as his former Real Madrid teammate Luka Modric moved to AC MIlan in the summer.

Modric turned 40 in September and joined Milan on a one-year contract, with the option of a one-year extension.

Football.title: Chelsea files: Estevao snubs Premier League rivals, Blues’ exciting youth project and damning red card stat
Football.content:
ESTEVAO WILLIAN snubbed a number of Premier League rivals to join Chelsea because of the “perfect future” they offered.

The Blues’ Brazilian sensation, 18, moved from Palmeiras to Stamford Bridge this summer, but his family and representatives had met a number of other English clubs.





Chelsea agreed their £29million deal for the wonderkid in 2024, but Manchester United, Tottenham and Nottingham Forest all met to discuss a move.

Estevao said Chelsea’s “project” convinced him they were the right club to join as he aims to be one of the world’s greatest players.

The Brazil international grew up idolising Neymar and wants to emulate his hero one day.

Estevao told SunSport: “My family went to other clubs but what caught my attention was Chelsea.



“The project with Chelsea was different because it’s a team that, how can I say, It gives you more opportunities to be in the game.

“I thought Chelsea would be the perfect future for me because it would get me more minutes.

“I want to be amongst the best players in the world.

“I know my potential and what I can offer to Chelsea and of course to be amongst the best players in the world.”



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

Estevao did not grow up watching much Premier League football, but kept a keen eye on Brazilians across Europe – with Neymar a clear favourite.

Asked who his idol was, he added: “Neymar. You’ll see if I can achieve that [level].”

The teenage winger is one of a number of young stars the Blues have snapped up since Clearlake Capital and Todd Boehly bought the club in 2022, with fellow Brazilian Andrey Santos among them.

The Chelsea Blueprint on YouTube Calling all Chelsea fans - we have a brand new YouTube channel! The Sun’s Chelsea YouTube channel will be your go-to destination for the latest news, transfer updates, match previews, post-match analysis, exclusive interviews, fan reactions, and breaking stories from Stamford Bridge. Subscribe to The Chelsea Blueprint on YouTube now

Santos, alongside compatriot Joao Pedro, is helping Estevao settle in London and both often operate as translators for the winger.

Estevao is having regular English lessons which appear to be bearing fruit.



The teenager has become so confident in the progress he is making that he allowed TV cameras into one of his lessons with the Blues regular languages tutor at their Cobham base last week.

“UNTOUCHABLES” TEST

ONE big concern Chelsea fans have with life under the current ownership is that every single player has a price tag.

While that is true of almost all clubs, Blues insiders insist there are a few untouchables at Stamford Bridge.

Their model involves a higher level of player trading than many of their rivals, with an emphasis on youth.



Players such as Renato Veiga, Noni Madueke and Kiernan Dewsbury-Hall have come and gone in recent years with mixed levels of success.

But a core principal of the plan, Chelsea insist, is that the biggest and brightest talents such as Cole Palmer and Moises Caicedo, will not be allowed to move on.

That stance is yet to be tested as no bids have been received for their brightest lights.

Things would become far more difficult if a Real Madrid came knocking, as they might do for Enzo Fernandez in the summer, or were the Blues to miss out on Champions League football.

In the latter scenario, the club’s wage bill would fall because player contracts are incentivised, rewarding them for reaching the CL but taking that boost away if they don’t qualify.



The overall message is Chelsea are on a stable financial footing, despite the bad headlines around the British record loss of £355m reported by Uefa last week.

The Blues are now making operating profits which means they do not need to make profits from player trading in order to comply with Uefa and Premier League financial rules.

But the reality is players like Fernandez, Palmer and Caicedo regard themselves as CL-level players and could put pressure on for a move, regardless of their long-term contracts.

SATPAEV’S MENTOR



NIGEL JAMES, the father of Chelsea captain Reece and women’s star Lauren James, will be involved in the development of one of Chelsea’s most promising prospects.

Nigel announced on Instagram that his coaching team have been trusted with the support and development of Kazakh superstar Dastan Satpaev, who is set to join Chelsea in the summer.

A post with pictures of Satpaev and Nigel was captioned: “@nigeljameselitecoaching has been trusted with the support and mentorship of the talented Dastan Satpaev.

“The talented youngster is set to join Chelsea from FC Kairat next season. The 17-year-old is the most exciting talent to emerge from Kazakhstan.

“Exciting times ahead for Dastan, us, and Chelsea FC.”



Fans were pleased with the announcement, and it is no secret how involved Nigel was with the development of current Chelsea stars Reece and Lauren.

Satpaev will join Chelsea in the summer, alongside Geovany Quenda, Emanuel Emegha, Denner Evangelista and the returning Mike Penders on loan at Strasbourg.

As revealed previously in the Chelsea Files, the working relationship between the Blues and Kairat is excellent, with the Kazakh club paying a visit to Cobham in January.

JUST KEEP 11 MEN ON THE PITCH!



LIAM ROSENIOR is as unhappy as anyone at Chelsea’s poor discipline.

Sorry Liam, but we’ve got some stats that will make you a little more miserable.

Or, they might just give the Chelsea boss and Blues fans a little hope for the future…

The data, from markstats.club, shows that based on expected goals compared to expected goals allowed, the Stamford Bridge outfit are competing with Arsenal as the best team in the league.

The kicker being, the data shown is explicitly measured with ELEVEN players on the pitch, not 10 – with Pedro Neto becoming the NINTH Chelsea player to be sent off this season in Sunday’s 2-1 defeat at the Emirates.



The Blues then, are one of the league’s finest at 11v11.

Chelsea have the highest expected goals in the Premier League according to this data, with their expected goals allowed only behind the Gunners and Newcastle.

Rosenior’s side have got a league-high expected goal stat of 53.4 goals, followed by Man City in second with 51.1 xG.

The not-so-good news is that they are underperforming their xG, and are the league’s fourth highest scorers, behind Man City, Arsenal and Manchester United.

Rosenior and Chelsea fans will be hoping that their deficit is paid up in a tough run of games to come, where defences won’t sit so deep against the Blues.



BIG BEN BLUES?

IF Chelsea had had their way, Benjamin Sesko might have been playing up top for them right now instead of Manchester United.

Blues chiefs and fans are probably happy to have Joao Pedro as their No 9 right now, as the Brazilian is outscoring the big Slovenian.

But Chelsea’s pursuit of Sesko back in 2022 should be seen in the context of Todd Boehly’s brief spell as sporting director after he fronted the Stamford Bridge takeover in May of that year.



By his own admission, Boehly knew little about players and the market, and the Blues went for Brighton’s Marc Cucurella mainly because Manchester City were in for the Spaniard, too.

Sources say Chelsea offered Sesko, then at Red Bull Salzburg, “whatever he wanted” to join them in that same summer.

United were also tracking the teenager, but those around him believed it was too soon to move to the Premier League.

A deal was struck for him to stay in Austria for one more year before joining sister club RB Leipzig.

Later in 2022 Chelsea hired Christopher Vivell from Leipzig as technical director, as Boehly and Co build a new recruitment team led by Paul Winstanley and Laurence Stewart.



Yet when the Blues raided Leipzig it was for Christopher Nkunku, not Sesko.

Rumours of a deal to activate Nkunku’s £56m release clause were rife in the autumn of 2022r and the transfer went through the following summer, along with the £30m capture of Nicolas Jackson.

Meanwhile Vivell’s time at Chelsea was up as the club placed him on gardening leave in July 2023.

The German joined United as interim director of recruitment ahead of the 2024-5 season and the position was made permanent in February 2025.

United signed Sesko for £74m in the summer, while Chelsea paid £60m for Pedro, spent another £30m on Liam Delap and recouped £36m for flop Nkunku from AC Milan.



The outcome of the race for the Champions League will be one way of measuring which club did the better business.

CHIN UP, REGGIE

REGGIE WALSH was called back to speak to Chelsea Under-21s coach Harry Hudson on the pitch after the Blues side beat their Manchester United counterparts 2-0 on Monday.

It appeared as though Hudson was trying to midfielder’s head up in a long conversation after hewas subbed off in the 73rd minute for Harrison McMahon.



Walsh, who was a standout performer on the night, then turned to take pictures with several young local fans.

The key moment of the game saw Walsh’s brilliant pinpoint cross headed in by Leo Cardoso in the first-half after Shumaira Mheuka had netted a slick opener for the visitors

An impressive team performance saw Chelsea leapfrog an injury ravaged United to go top of the PL2 table with four games remaining until the play-offs.

The win was a welcome boost for the Blues’ Academy after the FA Youth Cup defeat by Manchester City and elimination by Real Madrid in the last 16 of the Uefa Youth League.

Walsh and Mheuka, who have both already played for the first team, are among the brightest hopes at Cobham.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

They share an October 20 birthday and will turn 18 and 19 respectively later this year.

Football.title: Five Dream Team defenders have passed 200 points this season and two of them feature in fewer than 4% of teams
Football.content:
JUST five defenders have achieved 200+ points in Dream Team this season.

This elite group is made up of Jurrien Timber (£3.9m), Gabriel (£4.9m), Marc Guehi (£4.4m), Matheus Nunes (£4.8m) and Neco Williams (£4.6m).



While these five players are bonded as double centurions, there is a clear divide in terms of popularity.



Whereas Timber, Gabriel and Guehi are all among the ten most selected assets in the game, both Nunes and Williams currently appear in fewer than 4% of teams.

DREAM TEAM’S TOP DEFENDERS

243 pts – Jurrien Timber (44.1% ownership)

223 pts – Gabriel (21.7%)

217 pts – Marc Guehi (40.1%)

202 pts – Matheus Nunes (3.6%)

202 pts – Neco Williams (3.4%)

To a certain degree, it’s understandable why gaffers have been reluctant to back Nottingham Forest’s No3.

The East Midlands club are onto their fourth permanent head coach of the season and remain at risk of relegation in 17th, two points ahead of West Ham in the drop zone.

However, Forest’s blunt attack has been their biggest issue — only Wolves have scored fewer league goals at this stage.



Defensively, they’ve conceded fewer goals than eight teams, including Fulham, Bournemouth and Newcastle.

Therefore it’s fair to ascertain that Forest’s defenders are considerably more viable than their attack-minded team-mates.

Williams arguably produced his worst performance of the season at Brighton on Sunday but he’s done the work of two men for most of the campaign.



The Wales international ranks first among all Dream Team defenders for tackles (100) and shots on target (19).

And only two players in his position – Lewis Hall (£4.1m) and Ezri Konsa (£4.1m) – have earned more bonus points.

Having beaten Fenerbahce over two legs in the play-off round, Forest remain active in the Europa League, a useful source of points for Williams and company.

Vitor Pereira’s troops are due to face Midtjylland in the last 16 across Gameweeks 27 and 28.



As for Nunes, he’s been curiously underrated all season long.

A midfielder by trade, the Portugal international has been converted into Pep Guardiola’s first-choice right-back.

Rotation-proof Man City players should be cherished and so it’s hard to explain why Dream Team bosses have been cold on Nunes given only Erling Haaland (£7.2m) and Bernardo Silva (£3.7m) have played more league minutes among outfielders.

The 27-year-old has registered seven assists in all competitions, a tally only bettered by Reece James (£4.7m) among defenders — Chelsea’s captain was deployed as a midfielder more often than not before Enzo Maresca’s departure.

He has also contributed to 14 clean sheets, only Arsenal’s top dogs rank higher in the key metric at the time of writing.



Nunes and Guehi both average an impressive 5.9 points-per-game this season and yet the difference between the two in terms of popularity is gargantuan.

Dream Team managers in need of difference-makers should seriously consider Nunes as an option with City scheduled to fulfil six fixtures across the next three Gameweeks (not including this Wednesday’s home against Forest to complete Gameweek 26).

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Without over-complicating things, all one has to do is look at Williams and Nunes’ returns to see they have been quality performers this season.

It’s time more gaffers realised it.

Football.title: Fifa quietly bring back hated rule made famous by former England manager ahead of World Cup 2026
Football.content:
FIFA has quietly reintroduced a rule on substitutions that was hated by football fans.

National team managers are now able to make eight substitutions in a friendly.





And if the bosses of both teams agree, they can make up to 11 per game.

The rule was last in place over two decades ago but a change was made when former England boss Sven-Goran Eriksson received backlash for his management of the squad.

Eriksson changed his entire Three Lions team at half-time during a friendly against Australia at Upton Park in 2003.

Mass substitutions were banned as a result of the backlash.

The reintroduction of the substitution rule comes amid a series of rule changes made ahead of the World Cup this year.

The International Football Association Board (IFAB) announced the adjustments last week.

They include the newly-introduced use of VAR for checking corners as well as cards awarded to the wrong team and second yellow cards.

The IFAB also announced a set of new rules designed to increase the pace of games by countering tactics that can be used to timewaste.



CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

Referees will be given the power to start a five-second countdown if they feel players are taking too long over throw-ins and goal kicks.

In addition, substitutions must be completed in no more than 10 seconds, otherwise the substitute must stay off the pitch for another minute.

Eriksson tragically passed away aged 76 after revealing he had terminal pancreatic cancer.

The Swede spent five years as the Three Lions’ boss – taking England to the quarter-finals in two World Cups and a European Championship.

Football.title: Man Utd new-look XI next season with £150m double Premier League swoop
Football.content:
Man Utd new-look XI next season with £150m double Premier League swoop

Manchester United will be active in the summer as they aim to excel in the Premier League next season

View 3 Images Could United secure both Bruno Gumairaes and Sandro Tonali? (Image: Visionhaus, Visionhaus/Getty Images)

Manchester United might bolster their current squad with a duo of Newcastle midfielders in the summer, aiming to reclaim their position at the top of the Premier League. United remain undefeated under Michael Carrick, securing six victories from their seven matches since he took over from Ruben Amorim on a temporary basis.

‌



New signings are being mapped out for the summer across both the coaching staff and first-team line-up. Supporters are advocating for Carrick's permanent appointment while potential transfers are being suggested for various roles.

‌



With Casemiro set to leave in the summer, Bruno Guimaraes and Sandro Tonali have been identified as prime candidates to fill the Brazilian's shoes. AS reports that the Magpies pair are high on United's midfield wishlist, alongside Nottingham Forest's Elliot Anderson.

‌



Crystal Palace's Adam Wharton and Wolves star Joao Gomes are also viewed as targets for a revamped midfield, with a substantial transfer budget allocated for Casemiro's replacement. It's believed that around £175million will be spent, giving United the freedom to invest heavily in their preferred options.

Michael Carrick told what Sir Alex Ferguson trait he must copy to land Man Utd job READ MORE :

Amad hints at Man Utd exit in concerning post days after being dropped for Benjamin Sesko READ MORE :

Initial, informal contact has reportedly already been established with both players and Casemiro himself has singled out Guimaraes as his successor within the team. Newcastle are rumoured to be negotiating new contracts with both players, but no extension has been finalised as yet.

Ensure our latest sport headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

‌



Should Guimaraes and Tonali complete the Premier League move to Old Trafford, the permanent boss will face a challenge in determining their revised midfield set-up. Captain Bruno Fernandes has previously been mentioned in transfer speculation but United are planning discussions to offer him fresh terms in a bid to ward off suitors.

Academy prospect Kobbie Mainoo appeared destined for a loan departure under Amorim but has rediscovered success following Carrick's appointment. He is yet to receive a new contract though it is understood that United are locking in conversations to prolong his agreement beyond 2027.

View 3 Images The Red Devils are interested in the Magpies duo (Image: Jean Catuffe, Getty Images)

‌



The midfield isn't the only transformation anticipated this summer, with United keen on acquiring left-winger Yan Diomande from RB Leipzig. The 19-year-old features on their recruitment shortlist with Paris Saint-Germain and additional Premier League sides showing interest in the Bundesliga talent.

Diomande has registered 16 goal contributions thus far this campaign and Leipzig are likely selling the teenager in the summer. Marcus Rashford is currently on loan at Barcelona and might depart his boyhood team for a fresh opportunity in Spain.

This leaves United lacking on the left with Matheus Cunha, Patrick Dorgu and even Bryan Mbeumo being pushed onto that flank for the moment. With left-wing and central midfield addressed, United could go all out and recruit another striker to rival the in-form Benjamin Sesko.

‌



View 3 Images Yan Diomande is also wanted by United (Image: Selim Sudheimer, Getty Images)

The Slovenia international has hit the ground running under Carrick, scoring seven times in his last eight outings. He will require reinforcement next season, with United competing for European football and an extended cup run compared to this campaign.

Rasmus Hojlund could come back from his loan stint at Napoli to offer assistance to Sesko after experiencing decent form in Italy. Crystal Palace's Jean-Philippe Mateta was mentioned in January with the striker reportedly 'hoping' that the transfer would come to fruition.

‌

Man United's dream starting XI for 2026/27 season

Lammens; Dorgu, Maguire, Martinez, Dalot; Tonali, Guimaraes, Fernandes; Diomande, Sesko, Mbeumo.

Substitutes: Bayindir, Shaw, Mainoo, Diallo, Cunha, Mateta, Hojlund.

Football.title: Football fan 'stabbed' by wife who thought VAR fury was insult directed at her
Football.content:
Football fan 'stabbed' by wife who thought VAR fury was insult directed at her

A football fan reportedly ended up in hospital after allegedly becoming a victim of a violent assault while watching a match

View 2 Images A man was allegedly stabbed by his wife during Napoli's match against Atalanta (Image: Getty Images)

A woman allegedly stabbed her football-obsessed husband after misinterpreting his rants about VAR as personal insults directed at her. The 40-year-old man, from Naples, was watching Napoli play against Atalanta last month when the violent incident reportedly occurred, according to foreign press reports.

El Tiempo states that the situation escalated after the fan started shouting at the television when the reigning Serie A champions had a penalty decision reversed by VAR.

However, it's reported that the wife mistakenly thought the insults were targeted at her, with EFE news agency reporting: "His 35-year-old wife interpreted his words as being directed at her, threatened her partner, and when he refused to leave the house, she first threw a pair of scissors at him and then several kitchen knives."

Local media suggest she then allegedly shouted at him: "Leave or I'll stab you!" Gazzetta reports that after an unsuccessful attempt to stab her husband with the knife, she managed to wound him in his side.

Victor Osimhen's secret contract clause after Man Utd snub and Liverpool name dropped READ MORE :

Five Man Utd transfer blunders that gave club highest net spend in the WORLD READ MORE :

Despite bleeding, the man was reportedly able to call emergency services but remained in danger as his wife continued to hurl knives at him, one of which ended up lodged in their living room wall. When police arrived, they are said to have halted the attack and searched the woman, discovering three additional knives in her bag.

View 2 Images Antonio Conte's side fell to a 2-1 defeat to Atalanta last month (Image: Getty Images)

Article continues below

Whilst the 40-year-old is said to have suffered multiple injuries, none were life-threatening and he remains in hospital recovering. His wife was later arrested and is still being held in custody whilst facing assault charges.

The match in Bergamo finished in the hosts' favour with Atalanta securing a 2-1 victory over the champions courtesy of strikes from Mario Pasalic and Lazar Samardžić. The loss marked Napoli's sixth defeat of the campaign as their title hopes continue to fade, with league leaders Inter Milan holding a 10-point advantage over second-placed AC Milan.

Napoli did return to winning form a week afterwards when they secured a dramatic 2-1 away triumph at Hellas Verona. Rasmus Hojlund, who is on loan at the club from Manchester United, got the opener before his early goal was levelled by Jean-Daniel Akpa Akpro.

Romelu Lukaku proved to be the match-winner when he scored in the 97th minute to secure all three points for his team.

Football.title: Gabby Logan breaks down in tears over her father and heartbreaking phone call
Football.content:
Gabby Logan breaks down in tears over her father and heartbreaking phone call

BBC Sport presenter Gabby Logan became emotional while sharing a childhood memory of her late father Terry Yorath, the Welsh football legend who died in January aged 75

View 2 Images Gabby Logan became emotional when recollecting a childhood memory involving her father. (Image: These Three Things/YouTube)

Gabby Logan became visibly emotional whilst discussing her late father Terry Yorath on a podcast, with the BBC Sport presenter revealing how she felt the need to "protect" him during her childhood.

‌



Welsh football legend Yorath passed away in hospital in January, aged 75, after a brief illness, leaving Logan and her family devastated. Tributes flooded in for the Leeds United icon from around the globe, with his daughter describing him as a "warrior on the football pitch and a kind-hearted and generous man off it".

‌



Speaking on the These Three Things podcast with Deborah Thomas, Logan opened up about elements of her family life and personal relationships and became tearful as she shared a childhood memory involving her father.

‌



Amad hints at Man Utd exit in concerning post days after being dropped for Benjamin Sesko READ MORE :

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

Whilst talking about managing parenting in the spotlight, the 52-year-old Match of the Day presenter recollected how some lads at her son Reuben's school had looked up "some horrible things" about her on the internet, with Reuben choosing not to inform her about it "to protect her".

Confessing she was "mortified" by the situation, Logan explained that she wanted to emphasise to her children that protecting them was her responsibility and not theirs, with the episode triggering another recollection from her younger years. "That's a horrible feeling as a child, when you realise that your parent is not kind of up to it on some level," she explained.

‌



"I remember my dad asking me to get my mum a Mother's Day card. He was living in Vancouver because he was playing there and we were due to follow him, so he hadn't been home for a few months and he said: 'It's Mother's Day on Sunday, there's some money next to my bed.'

"But it wasn't Mother's Day," she added. "It was Mother's Day in North America but it wasn't in the UK.

View 2 Images Terry Yorath died in January after a short illness. (Image: Mark Leech/Offside via Getty Images)

‌



Becoming emotional, Logan added: "I remember getting off the phone and cry... crying my eyes out because I remember thinking 'Oh my dad's like, he doesn't know". And I thought he knew everything. And I think I missed him as well.

"I remember my mum telling me I had to stay off school and asking me 'What's wrong with you?' and I said 'Oh I've got a bad stomach' and I didn't.

"I just was heartbroken for him because he felt so out of the loop and he didn't feel like he was in our family anymore because he's been living in another country.

‌



"I remember that feeling so vividly and not wanting my kids to feel like they had to protect me," she added.

"So it was a good opportunity to let [Reuben] know he didn't need to be my saviour, and he didn't have to keep things to himself."

Football.title: Spanish media deliver brutal Real Madrid verdict before Man City clash as 'crisis' continues
Football.content:
Spanish media deliver brutal Real Madrid verdict before Man City clash as 'crisis' continues

Real Madrid's 1-0 defeat to Getafe has plunged the club into crisis ahead of their Champions League knockout tie against Manchester City

View 2 Images Real Madrid were defeated 1-0 by Getafe on Monday night (Image: Getty)

Real Madrid suffered a 1-0 defeat to mid-table Getafe on Monday, losing crucial ground in the La Liga title race. Their predicament has been labelled a "crisis" with Champions League knockout ties against Manchester City looming.

‌



Los Blancos parted company with manager Xabi Alonso in January following what they deemed underwhelming performances, with former player Alvaro Arbeloa taking charge. However, fortunes haven't turned around. They couldn't avoid the play-off round in the Champions League and their battle with rivals Barcelona for domestic supremacy in Spain has faltered in recent weeks.

‌



The 1-0 loss this week, which saw them jeered off by their own supporters and Franco Mastantuono dismissed in added time, follows an unexpected 2-1 defeat away at Osasuna. They now sit four points behind the Catalan giants.

‌



With Pep Guardiola's side scheduled to visit the Spanish capital on March 11 and host Real Madrid at the Etihad on March 17, City's opponents are in disarray and missing star striker Kylian Mbappe. Spanish press didn't hold back with their assessments following this latest blow.

MAKE THE MIRROR YOUR FIRST CHOICE! Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

Antonio Rudiger avoids punishment despite footage showing knee to the face READ MORE :

'Angry' Kylian Mbappe suffers huge Real Madrid blow ahead of Man City showdown READ MORE :

Marca

The forthcoming encounter with City has been portrayed as Real Madrid's intimidating cup final by Marca. Having characterised the current circumstances as a "crisis", a report read: "Real Madrid continues their freefall.

‌



"What seemed like a turnaround just two weeks ago has turned out to be a mirage and the season has once again become a disaster. The big test is now in the Champions League, where there's a feeling that Real Madrid's season hinges on it. The tie against Manchester City is a true final. A final that could be the end.

"Approaching that match with doubts, lacking confidence and with the atmosphere in turmoil doesn't seem like the best approach. And let's not forget, Mbappe is out of action."

‌



AS

Tomas Roncero of AS took it a step further. He penned: "Until today [Monday], I dreamed of a heroic double: La Liga and the Champions League. Well, I'm sorry to disappoint you, but my age prevents me from being unfaithful to my heart and my tormented mind.

"Believing that this Madrid team will beat City and overcome this four-point deficit with Barca is an act of faith. But faith has to be worked for, earned, nurtured. That's not the case here."

View 2 Images Mbappe is currently sidelined (Image: AP)

‌



His article also mentioned Mbappe's absence. The French superstar is dealing with a knee injury. Whilst the club have ruled out surgery, the player is reportedly unhappy with how his condition has been managed and is seeking a second opinion, undergoing tests in Paris.

The report continues: "It's clear that Kylian himself or the doctors haven't handled this knee sprain wisely and he's sorely missed. His goals were a lifeline for us. If this is going to be a long-term problem, we're in trouble."

Sport

Sport also emphasised the significance of the Champions League clashes with City, describing them as potentially pivotal occasions for manager Arbeloa. A report states: "Real Madrid fans are fed up with humiliation and are no longer staying silent.

‌



FOLLOW OUR REAL MADRID FB PAGE! Latest news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

"The supporters of the club have endured a drought of titles, good football and connection with the players over the last season and a half. It doesn't matter who the coach is: Carlo Ancelotti, Xabi Alonso, Alvaro Arbeloa... When none of them can find the right formula, the blame begins to fall on others.

Article continues below

"The tie against Manchester City is shaping up to be decisive and a turning point. And we're not just talking about the season; we're talking about an entire project and how it's being managed."

Football.title: Tottenham 'consider action' as Arsenal supporter staff member cracks jokes about club
Football.content:
Tottenham 'consider action' as Arsenal supporter staff member cracks jokes about club

Neros Coachman announced that he had joined Tottenham as an academy scout before cracking jokes about the fact he supports rivals Arsenal in the comments section

View 2 Images Neros Coachman made jokes about Tottenham after joining the club (Image: Instagram/@itzcoachman)

Tottenham have been left irritated after an Arsenal-supporting new member of staff cracked jokes about the club on social media. Comments by Neros Coachman were spotted by Spurs fans and have now reached the club's hierarchy, who are reportedly considering action.

‌



Coachman has joined Spurs as an academy scout, despite being an Arsenal fan. He shared the news on social media, drawing attention to his allegiances.

‌



He wrote on Instagram: "Its official. I'm now here, I sat and had the conversation and the project was exciting to say the least. Now looking forward to getting the new set of superstars to the club. You know the projects good when your a boyhood Arsenal fan and you switch sides."

‌



But it was the comments underneath the post that left a bitter taste in the mouth for some. One reply read: "Do not let that badge touch your skin," to which Coachman responded: "I've already got the base layer."

Another commenter said: "We need someone on the inside," with Coachman replying "shhhhh". Someone else then posted a picture of a snake and he said: "I know, I know, it’s deserved haha."

His words were quickly picked up on by Tottenham fans, who shared them on X. A post by The Spurs Express, which has now been viewed nearly 500,000 times, reads: "It is quite bizarre that, seemingly, a newly appointed member of staff at #Tottenham, who is a strong Arsenal fan, is openly mocking the club and joking about not wanting the badge to touch his skin, how 'it was tough' to put on Tottenham attire, and how he’ll be an 'inside man'. These are jokes, of course, but it doesn’t seem particularly befitting, given that he has taken up a role at #Spurs."

‌



The Daily Mail reports that they have got back to the club, who are 'deeply unimpressed' and considering action against Coachman, who has reacted by making his Instagram account private, but not before his response on his Instagram story was screenshotted.

View 2 Images Tottenham lost 4-1 to Arsenal recently (Image: David Price, Arsenal FC via Getty Images)

He wrote: "This is wild. Yes I'm and Arsenal Fan And yes I was joking with friends. As an arsenal fan of course I was joking about with friends regarding putting on a spurs kit.."

‌



Coachman's remarks have not come at a good time, with Tottenham enduring a miserable season and having recently lost 4-1 to Arsenal. The team sits 16th the Premier League, four points above the relegation zone, having not won in 10 league matches.

Igor Tudor has been brought in to replace Thomas Frank but has started with back-to-back defeats in the Premier League. They host Crystal Palace on Thursday before turning their attention to the last 16 of the Champions League when they face Atletico Madrid on Tuesday in the first leg.

Football.title: Jeremy Jacquet undergoing surgery on injury that left Liverpool signing screaming in agony
Football.content:
Stade Rennais have confirmed that Jeremy Jacquet will undergo surgery on a shoulder injury. The young defender, who will join up with Liverpool in the summer, picked up the problem last month and it has now been confirmed by he will be forced to have an operation.

The French club released a statement which read: "Jérémy Jacquet is set to undergo surgery On the pitch against Lens on February 7th, Jérémy Jacquet was forced to leave the game early due to a left shoulder injury.

"Following medical examinations, surgery has been scheduled in the coming days. Stade Rennais F.C. wishes him a speedy recovery."

Arne Slot's side completed a deal worth £60m for Jacquet in the latter stages of the January transfer window but for the 2026/27 season with the Frenchman remaining at Rennes until then.

However, just a few days later Jacquet picked up the bad injury and was forced off midway through the second half of the Ligue 1 defeat to Lens.

The 20-year-old was left screaming in agony after picking up the issue and was helped off the pitch by physios with his left shoulder supported.

Liverpool will be hoping the bulk of his recovery and rehabilitation process is completed by the time pre-season arrives with Slot in desperate need of a new centre-back.

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Ibrahima Konate sees his current contract expire in the summer and he is yet to sign a new deal while Virgil van Dijk will be 35 in a few months.

Liverpool tried to sign Marc Guehi ahead of the current campaign but failed to do so and he later joined Manchester City.

Young central defender Giovanni Leoni did join the club back in August but he picked up a serious knee injury on his debut which is likely to keep him out until the latter stages of 2026.

Image: Michael Zemanek/Shutterstock) Michael Zemanek/Shutterstock)

Alexander Isak, Conor Bradley and Wataru Endo are other long-term absentees for Slot with Florian Wirtz also still unavailable because of a back injury.

On the latter, Slot revealed on Monday: "The game tomorrow [vs Wolves] will probably come too soon, and maybe the game in the weekend as well, but let's see how it ends up. But we hope to have him back somewhere next week."

Football.title: Arsenal star reveals his big problem with London after hinting he wants to leave
Football.content:
Arsenal star reveals his big problem with London after hinting he wants to leave

Arsenal's Riccardo Calafiori has been unable to get used to the insane London traffic since arriving in England and admits he doesn't even have a car because of it

View 2 Images Riccardo Calafiori has found aspects of London life difficult

Arsenal star Riccardo Calafiori admits the one aspect of London he simply cannot stomach is the immense amount of traffic - which has seen him give up even having a car.

‌



The Italian moved to England's capital from Italy, where he was living in a city with a much smaller population. As a result journeys can take quite a while and it is not something Calafiori has been able to acclimatise to.

‌



Arsenal's training base is right on the cusp of the M25 in London Colney, but the Emirates Stadium is some 15 miles inside the London ring road.

‌



Calafiori, being a single man without a young family, admits he wanted to sample the city living, but few of his team-mates share his want for the bright lights. As a result his drive to training is just shy of an hour and he cannot fathom some of the driving habits in England.

He told the Supernova podcast: "I don’t drive, but I get around with a driver. I prefer not to drive here. I don’t have a car, but I can drive. I don’t like how they drive here. They follow the rules too much and are a bit slow. Sometimes you see these big traffic jams for no reason. Unfortunately, that’s just how it is here. Everyone has their ways."

The Italian added: "I live quite far away from my teammates; nobody lives where I live. It’s a London thing. Most of them have families and kids and live in houses near the training ground. I’m younger, and on my own, so I prefer living in the city. Normally, we check in by 9.15. It takes about 50 minutes to get there."

‌



Calafiori remains a squad player at the Emirates, starting just over half of their Premier League games this season. He was signed from Bologna in the summer of 2024 but has already talked up a return to his homeland with Roma - the club where he began his career.

View 2 Images Riccardo Calafiori has talked up a return to Roma in the future

Calafiori could end up costing Arsenal £42million if all his add-ons are met, but he's already eyeing up an eventual move back to the Stadio Olympico. He had to leave Roma to kickstart his career, joining Swiss giants Basel and later returning to Serie A with Bologna.

‌



However he longs for a move back. "I'd like to return to Roma sooner or later," Calafiori told Corriere dello Sport. "We can't plan it, of course, but yes, I want to return."

He only made 18 appearances in the Italian capital before moving elsewhere. Arsenal's depth has been cited as a huge plus but the amount of competition is likely to leave certain players seeking regular minutes.

Football.title: Premier League given solution to corner chaos as refs challenged to make brave calls
Football.content:
Chaos at corners is very much under the microscope after another weekend where set pieces proved crucial, but the dark arts occurring in the 18-yard box isn't pleasing everyone.

Arsenal have become the masters with both their goals against Chelsea coming courtesy of corners. They've made set-piece goals their trademark but the jostling, holding, jockeying for space and impact on the goalkeeper is becoming increasingly questioned.

Liverpool boss Arne Slot says concedes that Premier League games are no longer a "joy to watch" and his "football heart doesn't like" the importance that is now placed on dead ball situations.

The traditionalists may argue he needs to let go of his utopia and respect their place in the game. Others will look to the referees and say they've been far too soft for far too long. Disallowing a goal for a push, when there are multiple shoves in a matters of seconds, has always courted controversy.

‌



Is that aspect of the game beyond saving or can the game's bosses actually ensure that dead-ball situations are fairly competed? New laws are always being considered and we ask whether serious change is needed.

Chelsea handed clear VAR verdict vs Arsenal as Premier League told mistake made READ MORE :

MAKE THE MIRROR YOUR FIRST CHOICE! Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

Mirror Football's writers have given their takes on what can be done - or not done - to reduce the noise around the chaos that exists every time a ball is swung into the penalty area.

Jeremy Cross

Call me old fashioned, but football is a physical game. At least it used to be, until modern times sanitised the national sport to reduce it to something on a par with crocket. Jostling ahead of a corner is nothing more than handbags.

‌



Let them get on with it, because all teams are guilty. But the authorities won't. They love to get involved in making football better, yet only succeed in making it worse.

View 4 Images Arsenal have continued to be the masters at scoring from dead ball situations (Image: Getty Images)

So, following the introduction of VAR, which has changed the sport to such a detrimental level, why don't the Premier League go the whole hog and stick a camera on each crossbar.

Employ someone to watch the footage ahead of free kicks and corners, he can liaise directly with the referee and inform him of who has committed the worst offence. Another stoppage is the last thing football needs, but it's a way too late to worry about that.

‌



Felix Keith

Let's be honest: the game is a mess at the moment. The introduction and continual increase in power of VAR has started a downward spiral and every Ifab meeting just makes things worse as they try to tweak themselves out of the mess they've made.

Grappling at corners is a fair way down my list of grievances, below the shambolic handball rule and lengthy VAR delays, but it has become fairly ridiculous. If referees suddenly started clamping down on it, by giving a few penalties for holding, would it stamp it out?

View 4 Images Chelsea were immediately remonstrating with the referee after they conceded from a corner

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



Probably not instantly, but surely after a while, players would realise it's no longer open season in the box from set pieces.

These things tend to veer between the two extremes and the current version is, in my opinion, preferable to the other end of the spectrum, where every slight hint of contact results in a penalty. But it would be nice to see the middle ground once again, even if - in the short term - it would mean more outrage and cries of "all we ask for is consistency!"

Andy Dunn

The solution to the unseemly scrapping at corner-kicks lies solely in the hands of the officials. Down the years, there has been a school of thought that believes goalkeepers have been an over-protected species. Maybe. And if that has been the case, then it is time to make it official.

Keepers should be reassured that if they are impeded in ANY way when dealing with a corner-kick, they will get a free-kick. If they know there is a good chance of opponents being penalised for the slightest contact, then perhaps they will have the confidence to come out and deal with corners that are sent into the six-yard area - just like Senne Lammens did at Everton recently.

‌



View 4 Images Dealing with corners is becoming more and more of a challenge for goalkeepers

If a keeper comes out and an opponent stands in front of him, give a free-kick. Harsh? Perhaps. But everyone is sick and tired of this mess and if it takes a refereeing bias towards the team defending the corner to sort it out, then fine.

Let’s put it this way, watch the rest of the Champions League and watch the World Cup - there will be none of these six-yard box shenanigans because the referees will not put up with them. And it will never be the defending team who gets penalised.

Sam Meade

I'm all for gaining a competitive advantage, gamesmanship has its hands over every professional sport, not just football. But the reason it is allowed to become so common is because referees lack the bravery to make a big call.

‌



We've seen them try and push initiatives in the past, but they last a matter of weeks after some bad press. The constant line that seems to get thrown around is 'if you give a penalty for that, then you'll give 10 every game'. Ok, great, so be it. Penalties are as big a deterrent we have in the game.

Refs needs to start policing corners far better. Yes its a physical game, but goalkeepers cannot be blocked off from getting to the ball. Simple as. Attackers cannot hold a shirt and use their opponent as some form of a boost into the air without getting penalised.

Football.title: Lord Allen resigns as BHA chair after six months in role
Football.content:
The British Horseracing Authority has announced that Lord Allen has resigned as the ruling body’s chair after less than a year in the post.

Lord Charles Allen was nominated for the position late in 2024 after a search and he officially took up the role last September.

In a statement confirming his resignation, Lord Allen said: "Horseracing is an amazing sport with great potential and over the last year I have met some incredibly passionate people who love the sport and who believe to survive and prosper that change is needed.

"I also want to pay tribute to the hardworking and dedicated BHA team. I wish the sport well for the future."

Lord Allen took the role after the member organisations of the BHA (Racecourse Association, Racehorse Owners Association, Thoroughbred Breeders' Association and licensed personnel) unanimously agreed that there should be a completely independent board and that the BHA should have a commercial remit.

The members, who currently nominate representatives to the board, have not been able to reach agreement between themselves on changing the rules of the BHA to allow the achievement of those two goals as a change requires unanimous support. Accordingly, Lord Allen has resigned.

David Jones, the senior independent director of the BHA, said: "The board would like to thank Charles for the time and effort that he has put into the role helping us to develop a vision that is aspirational and achievable, and we regret that we were unable to put in place at this time the governance changes that would have enabled us to progress this. I have enjoyed working with him and wish him continued success."

The BHA board will discuss and determine what interim arrangements will be put in place.

Football.title: Brook: England ready for 'pressure' India semi-final after 'niggly' wins
Football.content:
Captain Harry Brook feels England coming through "niggly" games earlier in the T20 World Cup will stand them in good stead for their blockbuster semi-final against host nation India in Mumbai on Thursday.

Captivate Captivate , which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. You can use the buttons below to amend your preferences to enable Captivate cookies or to allow those cookies just once. You can change your settings at any time via the This content is provided by, which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. You can change your settings at any time via the Privacy Options Unfortunately we have been unable to verify if you have consented to Captivate cookies. To view this content you can use the button below to allow Captivate cookies for this session only. Enable Cookies Allow Cookies Once

Brook's side stuttered to wins over Associate nations Nepal, Scotland and Italy in the first round around a meek defeat to fellow two-time champions West Indies.

They improved in the Super 8s while going undefeated, beating Sri Lanka, Pakistan - as the skipper struck a stunning 50-ball century - and then finally New Zealand having looked up against it needing 43 from the final 18 deliveries.

England's MVP of the tournament Will Jacks - who has won four Player of the Match awards - and T20 World Cup debutant Rehan Ahmed slammed an unbroken, match-winning stand of 44 from 16 balls against the Black Caps after opening pair Jos Buttler and Phil Salt struggled again, scoring a duck and two respectively.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Full highlights from Colombo as Will Jacks and Rehan Ahmed powered England to a four-wicket win over New Zealand in the T20 World Cup

I always look at T20 cricket as thinking about the reward rather than the risk. It’s a case of backing yourself and thinking about what good can come of it rather than what bad can come of it. I will never tell anyone off for taking the aggressive option – I don’t think I have a leg to stand on there!

Brook told Sky Sports Cricket's Ian Ward: "We are managing to scrape along in close games and that is the sign of a good side.

"I have learned you are never out of games. I know I have players with a lot of character, a willingness to win, wanting to win a lot, but also that calmness under pressure."

'If one of our batters comes off, the opposition are scratching their heads'

Buttler is averaging just 8.85 in the competition after five successive single-figure scores, while Salt's only knock of note was 62 in the Super 8s encounter with Sri Lanka.

However, Brook feels his top-order duo can fire versus India, a side England are meeting for the third straight T20 World Cup semi-final, after hammering them in Adelaide in 2022 before being thrashed by them in Guyana in 2024.

Image: Jos Buttler's scores during his T20 World Cup campaign so far

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Former England all-rounder Moeen Ali is backing Buttler to rediscover best form after yet another disappointing innings, against New Zealand

The Yorkshireman added: "We are all really excited. I have already heard a few lads say they wanted to play India anyway. It is going to be a massive occasion with a hell of a lot of pressure for both sides.

"India obviously have a hell of a lot of support and I'm not sure there are going to be many people behind us. It's going to be loud. Hopefully we can play our best cricket.

"I am excited for the lads up top. I feel they are one step closer to taking the game away from a side. I don't think there is any weakness in the top order at all. The lads have just struggled a little bit and not got the runs they always get.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Take a look at the best bits from Brook's 50-ball century against Pakistan at the T20 World Cup, a knock that featured 10 fours and four sixes

"I think Jos should be left alone. He is one of the best players to have ever played the game and I can see him coming good.

"It's awesome other lads have been able to step up. There have been performances throughout. We have so many powerful players in the top seven that if one comes off, the opposition are scratching their heads and don't really know where to go."

Jacks crucial for England after 'thanks-for-coming game'

Jacks has been instrumental for England, contributing 191 runs at No 7, including across the nerve-jangling wins over Nepal, Italy, Sri Lanka and New Zealand, as well as seven wickets with his off-spin.

Brook said of the Surrey all-rounder: "Jacks has been outstanding. To do that in his first proper World Cup playing every game is phenomenal.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player The best of Jacks' maiden T20 international fifty, an innings that rescued England against Italy and helped the team into the World Cup Super 8s

"We chatted to him in Sri Lanka [during the pre-World Cup tour] when he had a thanks-for-coming game. He didn't bowl, didn't bat and got the ball once or twice in the field.

"We said to him it was a role that gives you a lot of reward when you do win games and that you are playing this role or it is hard for you to get in the side.

"He has embraced that position and done exceptionally well. The character he has shown has been awesome."

'I want to make it as uncomfortable as possible for the batter coming in at all times'

Brook's ice-cool captaincy has also been a key component of England reaching their fifth straight T20 World Cup semi-final, so how has he tried to operate?

"We have had a lot of pressure games and the calmer you can stay as a captain helps in every facet of the game.

"You can see me holding on to the ball a bit longer. I want to have a conversation with the bowler, make sure they know what they are trying to bowl, make them think about one thing.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Brook gives Sky Sports Cricket's Ian Ward an insight into how he leads as a captain

"For example, with wide yorkers, I am not that bothered about the line you bowl. I just want you to hit the yorker length.

"Unless the batter is scooping, no one is going to hit you for four or six. You can set funky fields but if you nail your length it's usually one, dot or wicket.

"With us bowling a lot more spin now, we do try to get ahead of the over rate and slow the game down. It works better in certain situations than others but that is the beauty of playing spin. You can use the maximum amount of minutes you have got.

"I aways have options in my head. I tell the lads who is potentially going to be bowling the second over. Sometimes it changes, sometimes it doesn't. After that it's watch the game, see how it's playing out and try to get the most effective bowler in a situation.

"I want to make it as uncomfortable as possible for the batter coming in at all times."

Watch England vs India in the T20 World Cup semi-finals, at Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai, live on Sky Sports Cricket and Sky Sports Main Event from 1pm on Thursday (1.30pm first ball).

Football.title: Frustrated Rahm explains why he won't sign DP World Tour deal
Football.content:
Jon Rahm has accused the DP World Tour of "extorting players" and says he will not sign a deal that allows him to play on the circuit and in LIV Golf simultaneously under current conditions.

Unlike his Ryder Cup team-mate Tyrrell Hatton, Rahm was not among the eight DP World Tour members recently given conditional releases to appear in LIV Golf without facing fines.

The Spaniard has previously said he has "no intention" of paying the financial punishments for breaching membership guidelines by featuring in LIV events without permission, with his fines believed to be in the region of £2.5m.

Unless an agreement is reached, his eligibility for next year's Ryder Cup in Ireland could be in doubt.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Rich Beem explains the DP World Tour statement on eight members to have been granted permission to play in conflicting tournaments on the LIV Golf circuit in 2026 - but why not John Rahm?

The chief sticking point for Rahm is the number of DP World Tour events he has been asked to appear in.

'I don't like the situation and I'm not going to agree to it'

Two-time major champion and former world No 1 Rahm said ahead of LIV Golf Hong Kong from Thursday: "I don't like what [the DP World Tour] are doing currently with the contract they're having us sign.

"They're asking me to play a minimum of six events, and they dictate where two of those have to be, amongst other things that I don't agree with.

"I did tell them to lower that to four events, like the minimum says, and I'll sign tonight. They haven't agreed to that. I just refuse to play six events. I don't want to and that's not what the rules say.

Image: Rahm says he would 'gladly' pay to play in next year's Ryder Cup in Ireland

"I don't know what game they're trying to play right now, but it just seems they're using our impact in tournaments, fining us and trying to benefit both ways from what we have to offer.

"In a way they're extorting players like myself and young players that have nothing to do with the politics of the game, so I don't like the situation and I'm not going to agree to that."

The eight players granted LIV releases - Hatton, Laurie Canter, Thomas Detry, Tom McKibbin, Adrian Meronk, Victor Perez, David Puig and Elvis Smylie - have all accepted that their presence at tournaments benefits the DP World Tour and its members.

Conditions for the players' release include paying outstanding fines in full for breaching the DP World Tour's regulations, as well as withdrawing all pending appeals.

The group must also participate in additional stipulated DP World Tour tournaments and media activity.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Jamie Weir looks at John Rahm's comments on his frustrations with the DP World Tour and feels it's looking like the Spaniard might not be part of the next Ryder Cup team.

'I'll pay to play in Ryder Cup - but not to be a DP World Tour member'

Rory McIlroy previously said Rahm and Hatton should pay their fines and prove their commitment to Team Europe, after the furore of Team America being given money for their appearances at the 2025 tournament in New York.

McIlroy told reporters in January: "We went really hard on the Americans about being paid to play. We also said that we would pay to play in Ryder Cups. There's two guys that can prove it."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player In January, Rory McIlroy urged Jon Rahm and Hatton to pay their outstanding DP World Tour fines to prove how much it means to them to play for Europe in the Ryder Cup

On McIlroy's comments, Rahm added: "That statement would make a lot more sense if all 12 of us were being asked to pay, not only just the two of us.

"There's more intricacy that goes into this whole situation, right.

"While I understand why he's saying that, we all do it for the love of the game, it's a different situation than what we usually see.

"I'll gladly pay my way to go on the Ryder Cup, not have to pay to still be a member of the DP World Tour and fulfil a commitment that I'm fully willing to commit."

Football.title: Iran’s women’s team decline to sing national anthem before Asian Cup tie
Football.content:
Iran’s women’s football team declined to sing their national anthem ahead of their opening match of the Asian Cup in Australia on Monday, their first fixture since the war in the Middle East began.

Every member of the team stood silently, facing straight ahead, during the anthem prior to kick-off in their Group A match against South Korea, who went on to win 3-0 at the Gold Coast Stadium in Queensland. Iran’s head coach, Marziyeh Jafari, and her players declined to comment on either the war or the death of their long-serving leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, when asked by the media.

The Iranian team arrived in Australia to prepare for the tournament several days before air strikes began in the Middle East. They will face the host nation on Thursday, before playing their third and final group-stage match against the Philippines on Sunday, both at the same venue.

Iran’s players have been praised for their courage. “Our heart goes out to them and their families, it’s a difficult situation and it’s really brave of them to be able to be here and to perform,” said the Australia midfielder Amy Sayer. “They put on a really strong performance, even with the political climate that’s going on and the struggles that they might be going through.

“The best we can do to contribute is to just give them the best game of football [on Thursday] that we’re able and to show them the respect on the field. Hopefully the situation improves and they can keep staying safe in Australia.”

Iran are the only team from the Middle East to have qualified for the tournament, which runs until 21 March. Australia opened their Asian Cup campaign with a 1-0 victory against the Philippines in Perth on Sunday. In Group B, North Korea were 3-0 victors over Uzbekistan.

Football.title: Jennifer Echegini: ‘Winning the Wafcon is on another level. The pride I felt’
Football.content:
Being an integral figure in the distinguished history of Nigeria’s women’s team is an experience that will never dim in the mind of Jennifer Onyinyechi Echegini. Seven months on from beating the hosts Morocco in a pulsating Women’s Africa Cup of Nations final at Rabat’s Olympic Stadium, in the process winning a record 10th African title, “Joe”, as her Paris Saint-Germain teammates call Echegini – an acronym of her three initials – is yet to come down from her career high.

“Winning the Wafcon is on another level, you know?” the 24-year-old midfielder says from Paris. “The pride and the achievement that I felt … when you’re playing with a group of girls that you love and care for, it makes it even more special.”

Overcoming a 2-0 first-half deficit to win 3-2 took every ounce of skill, resilience and fortitude, as Echegini vividly recalls. “We kind of just went [into the second half] with the mindset that it’s all or nothing … you either give everything and maybe get something out of it, or you don’t fight, and you then look back, wishing you had. We just went out with that mindset and gave everything.”

It was the welcome home in Nigeria and the subsequent reception at Aso Rock in Abuja, the seat of Nigeria’s government, where the players were each promised $100,000 (£75,000) and a house for the win (a pledge the government is yet to redeem) that left Echegini truly overwhelmed. “I already knew Nigerians do things big, but when you experience it, it’s on another level,” she says. “Being someone who grew [up] outside Nigeria and not so much into the Nigerian culture, it’s always beautiful to see how things get done. I feel like I’m learning something every time I go home.

“It just shows, as a nation, how supportive everybody is. Without them, winning the tournament wouldn’t have been as special. It was amazing to celebrate things with the people you care about and with fans who care about you and support you.”

Born in the Dutch city of Nijmegen, Echegini moved to England at the age of 11 with her parents before relocating to the US, where she played college football at Mississippi State and Florida State universities. Echegini’s peripatetic life has played a decisive role in the person she has become. “It’s only affected me for the better,” she says. “I would not be who I am or where I am today if I hadn’t experienced such things. I’m very grateful for the moments I’ve had. It’s not the same as an average person who grows up in their home town and goes to their local university. I am very grateful for those experiences.”

View image in fullscreen Jennifer Echegini is known as ‘Joe’ by her Paris Saint-Germain teammates. Photograph: Gualter Fatia/Uefa/Getty Images

Before Randy Waldrum, the American who coached Nigeria at the last World Cup in Australia, gave Echegini her debut in 2022, playing for the Netherlands was what she regarded as a more “realistic” ambition. “I had no contact with Nigeria at all, knew little about it,” she told the EaglesTracker Podcast. “I lived in the Netherlands until I was 11 and playing for the Netherlands seemed more realistic.”

But there has been no looking back ever since Echegini scored her first goal for Nigeria in a 3-0 win over New Zealand in 2023, the same year in which she went to the World Cup in Australia, where Nigeria had an impressive outing, losing to eventual finalists England in the last 16.

To maintain Nigeria’s impressive streak of qualifying for every World Cup since it began in 1991, the Falcons will have to make the last four of this year’s Wafcon, which for a third successive occasion will be held in Morocco, beginning on 17 March. Echegini knows the holders cannot be complacent when defending their title, with the gap between Nigeria and other teams on the continent, once a huge gulf, continuing to narrow.

Echegini also believes Nigeria’s Wafcon preparations are in desperate need of an organisational upgrade. “Preparation time is very limited,” she says. “You have to be very careful with how well you prepare for things. Last year we didn’t prepare as much as we wanted, but we still won. We are a team, regardless of the obstacles thrown at us or the lack of preparation, which always prevails in the end.”

In regards to becoming contenders at next year’s World Cup, should Nigeria qualify as expected, Echegini sounds a note of warning. “We need to prepare very well. We need to figure some things out, so we can be better,” she says. “When you compete with teams that have all these facilities and, essentially, their lives are easier, you’re already at a disadvantage.

“I’m not somebody who wants to complain, because, at the end of the day, football is football. But you have to put your team in the best position to compete. I hope we are able to utilise every camp window, so we can be as strong as we can”. The Nigeria Football Federation would be wise to heed the counsel of one of their national team’s most impressive figures.
